api/Chaos.Collections.Specialized.QuadTr.title: Method Query | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Collections.Specialized.QuadTr.keywords: Method Query Namespace Chaos.Collections.Specialized Assembly Chaos.Collections.dll Query(IRectangle) Queries the QuadTree<T> for items within the specified bounds. public virtual IEnumerable<T> Query(IRectangle bounds) Parameters bounds IRectangle The query bounds Returns IEnumerable<T> All items contained within the specified bounds Remarks We use a custom iterator here to avoid a lot of overhead. By default, iterator blocks are converted into classes, so they are allocated on the heap. The typical recursive iteration of a QuadTree would result in the allocation of a new iterator every time we go depth+1 from current, which would result in hundreds of allocations every time we query the tree. Instead, only one instance of the custom iterator is needed. Query(ICircle) Queries the QuadTree<T> for items within the specified bounds. public virtual IEnumerable<T> Query(ICircle bounds) Parameters bounds ICircle The query bounds Returns IEnumerable<T> All items contained within the specified bounds Remarks We use a custom iterator here to avoid a lot of overhead. By default, iterator blocks are converted into classes, so they are allocated on the heap. The typical recursive iteration of a QuadTree would result in the allocation of a new iterator every time we go depth+1 from current, which would result in hundreds of allocations every time we query the tree. Instead, only one instance of the custom iterator is needed. Query(IPoint) Queries the QuadTree<T> for items at the specified point. public virtual IEnumerable<T> Query(IPoint point) Parameters point IPoint The point to query items at Returns IEnumerable<T> All items located at the specified point

api/Chaos.DarkAges.Definitions.LightLeve.title: Enum LightLevel | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.DarkAges.Definitions.LightLeve.keywords: Enum LightLevel Namespace Chaos.DarkAges.Definitions Assembly Chaos.DarkAges.dll Feel free to rename these to align with whatever you have configured in your light metadata. public enum LightLevel : byte Extension Methods ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T>(T, Action, Action<Exception>) ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T, TReturn>(T, Func<T, TReturn?>, Func<Exception, TReturn?>) Fields Dark_A Dark_B Darker_A Darker_B Darkest_A Darkest_B Light_A Light_B Lighter_A Lighter_B Lightest_A Lightest_B Remarks You can have all 12 values be in sequence of darkest to lightest (or vice versa), but this will constrain you to only having 1 light type per map. Alternatively, you could 2 different light types defined here, 6 values for each, which would allow you to define 2 different light types per map without needing to restart the server. Each set of 6 values would both be defined under the same light type in the metadata.

api/Chaos.DarkAges.Definitions.ServerMes.keywords: Enum ServerMessageType Namespace Chaos.DarkAges.Definitions Assembly Chaos.DarkAges.dll A byte switch as used for ServerOpCode.ServerMessage public enum ServerMessageType : byte Extension Methods ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T>(T, Action, Action<Exception>) ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T, TReturn>(T, Func<T, TReturn?>, Func<Exception, TReturn?>) Fields ActiveMessage Texts appears in the action bar and Shift+F AdminMessage Text is only in the action bar, and will not show up in Shift+F. In official this was used for admin world messages ClosePopup Closes opened pop-up windows. ScrollWindow, NonScrollWindow, WoodenBoard GroupChat Text appears in a puke-green color. In official this was used for group chat GuildChat Text appears in an olive-green color. In official this was used for guild chat NonScrollWindow Pops open a window with no scroll bar. In official this was used for perish lore OrangeBar1 Text is only in the action bar, and will not show up in Shift+F OrangeBar2 Text is only in the action bar, and will not show up in Shift+F OrangeBar3 Text is only in the action bar, and will not show up in Shift+F OrangeBar5 Text is only in the action bar, and will not show up in Shift+F PersistentMessage Text appears white, and persists indefinitely until cleared in the top right corner ScrollWindow Pops open a window with a scroll bar. In official this was used for Sense UserOptions UserOptions are sent via this text channel Whisper Text appears blue, and appears in the top left WoodenBoard Pops open a window with a wooden boarder. In official this was used for signposts and wooden boards

api/Chaos.Extensions.Geometry.PointExten.keywords: Method ConalSearch Namespace Chaos.Extensions.Geometry Assembly Chaos.Extensions.Geometry.dll ConalSearch(Point, Direction, int) Lazily generates a sequence of points in a cone shape. public static IEnumerable<Point> ConalSearch(this Point point, Direction direction, int maxDistance) Parameters point Point The starting point of the cone direction Direction The direction the cone is facing maxDistance int The maximum distance the cone extends from the starting point Returns IEnumerable<Point> An enumeration of points in the shape of a cone Remarks The maxDistance is the maximum distance the cone extends from the starting point, however there will be points that are part of the cone that are farther than maxDistance distance from the starting point. This is because the forward edges and the center of the cone both extend the same number of spaces in the given direction. api/Chaos.Extensions.Geometry.PointExten.title: Method GenerateCardinalPoints | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Extensions.Geometry.PointExten.keywords: Method GenerateCardinalPoints Namespace Chaos.Extensions.Geometry Assembly Chaos.Extensions.Geometry.dll GenerateCardinalPoints(Point, Direction, int) Lazily generates an enumeration of points in a line from the user, with an option for distance and direction. Direction.All is optional. Direction.Invalid direction returns empty list. public static IEnumerable<Point> GenerateCardinalPoints(this Point start, Direction direction = null, int radius = 1) Parameters start Point direction Direction radius int The max distance to generate points Returns IEnumerable<Point> Examples //generates points in a counter clockwise spiral around the start //will generate the 4 points immediately around the start var points = new Point(0, 0).GenerateCardinalPoints(); Remarks Assumes Chaos.Geometry.Abstractions.Definitions.Direction.Up is equivalent to the cardinal direction "North", this method will generate points in all 4 cardinal directions. Points will be generated 1 radius at a time, clock-wise. GenerateCardinalPoints(IPoint, Direction, int) public static IEnumerable<Point> GenerateCardinalPoints(this IPoint start, Direction direction = null, int radius = 1) Parameters start IPoint direction Direction radius int Returns IEnumerable<Point>

api/Chaos.Extensions.Geometry.PointExten.title: Method GenerateIntercardinalPoints | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Extensions.Geometry.PointExten.keywords: Method GenerateIntercardinalPoints Namespace Chaos.Extensions.Geometry Assembly Chaos.Extensions.Geometry.dll GenerateIntercardinalPoints(Point, Direction, int) Lazily generates an enumeration of diagonal points in relevance to the user, with an optional distance and direction. Direction.All is optional. Direction.Invalid direction returns an empty enumeration public static IEnumerable<Point> GenerateIntercardinalPoints(this Point start, Direction direction = null, int radius = 1) Parameters start Point direction Direction The general direction to generate points for. See remarks. radius int The range in which to generate points Returns IEnumerable<Point> Examples //this call will generate points in the inter-cardinal directions "North-West" and "North-East" //4 points will be generated, 2 in each inter-cardinal direction //the points will be diagonal to the original point var points = new Point(0, 0).GenerateInterCardinalPoints(Direction.Up, 2); //this call will generate points in the inter-cardinal directions "South-West" and "South-East" //10 points will be generated, 5 in each inter-cardinal direction //the points will be diagonal to the original point var points = new Point(0, 0).GenerateInterCardinalPoints(Direction.Down, 5); //this call will generate points in all inter-cardinal directions //12 points will be generated, 3 in each inter-cardinal direction //the points will be diagonal to the original point var points = new Point(0, 0).GenerateInterCardinalPoints(Direction.All, 3); Remarks Assuming Chaos.Geometry.Abstractions.Definitions.Direction.Up is equivalent to the cardinal direction "North", this method will generate points in the inter-cardinal directions "North-East", "South-East", "South-West", and "North-West". Points will be generated 1 radius at a time, clock-wise. Optionally, you can choose a cardinal Chaos.Geometry.Abstractions.Definitions.Direction to generate points for the 2 inter-cardinal directions that share the given cardinal direction. GenerateIntercardinalPoints(IPoint, Direction, int) public static IEnumerable<Point> GenerateIntercardinalPoints(this IPoint start, Direction direction = null, int radius = 1) Parameters start IPoint direction Direction radius int Returns IEnumerable<Point>

api/Chaos.IO.Definitions.Endianness.html.title: Enum Endianness | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.IO.Definitions.Endianness.html.keywords: Enum Endianness Namespace Chaos.IO.Definitions Assembly Chaos.IO.dll A value representing the endianness to use when reading/writing data public enum Endianness Extension Methods ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T>(T, Action, Action<Exception>) ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T, TReturn>(T, Func<T, TReturn?>, Func<Exception, TReturn?>) Fields BigEndian = 1 Big endian is a term used to describe the byte order of multi-byte data types in computer memory. In big endian, the most significant byte (MSB) is stored at the lowest memory address, and the least significant byte (LSB) is stored at the highest memory address. In other words, the bytes are ordered from the largest to the smallest, based on their significance. LittleEndian = 0 Little endian is a term used to describe the byte order of multi-byte data types in computer memory. In little endian, the least significant byte (LSB) is stored at the lowest memory address, and the most significant byte (MSB) is stored at the highest memory address. In other words, the bytes are ordered from the smallest to the largest, based on their significance.

WriteBoolean(bool) Writes a boolean value to the buffer. WriteByte(byte) Writes a byte value to the buffer. WriteBytes(params ReadOnlySpan<byte>) Writes an array of bytes to the buffer. WriteData(byte[], bool) Writes a byte array to the buffer with an optional line feed. WriteData16(byte[]) Writes a byte array with a prepended 16-bit length to the buffer. WriteData8(byte[]) Writes a byte array with a prepended 8-bit length to the buffer. WriteInt16(short) Writes a 16-bit signed integer value to the buffer. WriteInt32(int) Writes a 32-bit signed integer value to the buffer. WritePoint16(ushort, ushort) Writes two 16-bit unsigned integers as a point to the buffer. WritePoint8(byte, byte) Writes two 8-bit unsigned integers as a point to the buffer. WriteSByte(sbyte) Writes an 8-bit signed byte value to the buffer. WriteString(string, bool, bool) Writes a string to the buffer with optional line feed or null termination. WriteString16(string) Writes a string with a prepended 16-bit length to the buffer. WriteString8(string) Writes a string with a prepended 8-bit length to the buffer. WriteUInt16(ushort) Writes a 16-bit unsigned integer value to the buffer. WriteUInt32(uint) Writes a 32-bit unsigned integer value to the buffer. WriteValue<T>(T) Writes a value of a numeric type to the buffer.

api/Chaos.MetaData.ClassMetaData.Ability.title: Constructor AbilityMetaNode | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.MetaData.ClassMetaData.Ability.keywords: Constructor AbilityMetaNode Namespace Chaos.MetaData.ClassMetaData Assembly Chaos.MetaData.dll AbilityMetaNode(string, bool, BaseClass) A node that stores metadata about an ability. public AbilityMetaNode(string Name, bool IsSkill, BaseClass Class) Parameters Name string IsSkill bool Class BaseClass Remarks Each file has a name of SClass{Number} where Number is the byte representing the class the metadata file is for Peasant = 0, Warrior = 1, Rogue = 2, Wizard = 3, Priest = 4, Monk = 5, There is a node named "Skill" with no properties at the beginnin after all the skills, there is a node "Skill_End" with 1 property, but that property is blank "00 01 00 00" followed by 3 more 00s "00 00 00" then, there is a node "Spell" with 1 empty property "00 01 00 00" then when spells finished, "Spell_End" with 1 empty property "00 01 00 00" Node name = name of skill with rank property 1 = {RequiredLevel}/{IsMaster}/{RequiredAbility} property 2 = {SpriteId}/??/?? property 3 = {Str}/{Int}/{Wis}/{Dex}/{Con} property 4 = "{PreRequisite1}/{RequiredLevel}" property 5 = "{PreRequisite1}/{RequiredLevel}" - NOTE.. for no prereq, use "0/0" property 6 = {Description}

api/Chaos.Models.Data.ShardingOptions.Ex.title: Property ExitLocation | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Models.Data.ShardingOptions.Ex.keywords: Property ExitLocation Namespace Chaos.Models.Data Assembly Chaos.dll ExitLocation The instanceId to teleport players to if they log on and the instance no longer exists public Location ExitLocation { get; init; } Property Value Location Remarks Imagine a scenario where a players disconnects. They should be able to log back into the instance they were already in. Now imagine a scenario where a player logs off, then logs back on 5 hours later. The instance will likely no longer exist. We can't simply create a new instance for them, this would be abusable (bosses would respawn, events would reset, etc) This instanceid is the place to put people who are in this scenario, generally speaking it should be the map you enter this instance from

api/Chaos.Models.World.Abstractions.Crea.title: Method TraverseMap | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Models.World.Abstractions.Crea.keywords: Method TraverseMap Namespace Chaos.Models.World.Abstractions Assembly Chaos.dll TraverseMap(MapInstance, IPoint, bool, bool, Func<Task>?) Attempts to move this entity from its current map to the destination map public virtual void TraverseMap(MapInstance destinationMap, IPoint destinationPoint, bool ignoreSharding = false, bool fromWolrdMap = false, Func<Task>? onTraverse = null) Parameters destinationMap MapInstance destinationPoint IPoint ignoreSharding bool fromWolrdMap bool onTraverse Func<Task>? Remarks This method does not use the MapInstance.BeginInvoke api due to not knowing who the caller is. The caller could be from another map, which would require entrancy into this entity's map synchronization, but there is no way of knowing where the caller is from. In order for this to be safe, it has to make no assumptions and deal with every requirement itself.

api/Chaos.Networking.Abstractions.Defini.title: Enum ClientOpCode | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Networking.Abstractions.Defini.keywords: Enum ClientOpCode Namespace Chaos.Networking.Abstractions.Definitions Assembly Chaos.Networking.Abstractions.dll OpCodes used when receiving packets from a client public enum ClientOpCode : byte Extension Methods ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T>(T, Action, Action<Exception>) ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T, TReturn>(T, Func<T, TReturn?>, Func<Exception, TReturn?>) Fields BeginChant OpCode used when a client begins casting a spell with cast lines Hex value: 0x4D BoardInteraction OpCode used when a client accesses a board or mail Hex value: 0x3B Chant OpCode used when a client uses an ability that has chant lines Hex value: 0x4E Click OpCode used when a client clicks on an object Hex value: 0x43 ClientException OpCode used when a client experiences an exception caused by a bad packet Hex value: 0x42 ClientRedirected OpCode used when a client is redirected to this server Hex value: 0x10 ClientWalk OpCode used when a client walks in a direction Hex value: 0x06 CreateCharFinalize OpCode used when a client requests to create a new character. This is the second step in the process and will contain appearance details Hex value: 0x04 CreateCharInitial Opcode used when a client requests to create a new character. This is the first step in the process and will only contain a name and password Hex value: 0x02 DialogInteraction OpCode used when a client responds to a dialog Hex value: 0x3A DisplayEntityRequest OpCode used when a client requests the server to display an object Hex value: 0x0C EditableProfile OpCode used when a client responds to a request for profile data (portrait, text) Hex value: 0x4F Emote OpCode used when a client uses an emote Hex value: 0x1B ExchangeInteraction OpCode used when a client interacts with an exchange window Hex value: 0x4A ExitRequest OpCode used when a client tries to log out Hex value: 0x0B GoldDrop OpCode used when a client drops gold on the ground Hex value: 0x24 GoldDroppedOnCreature OpCode used when a client drops gold on a creature Hex value: 0x2A GroupInvite OpCode used when a client invites another player to a group, responds to a group invite, or creates or destroys a group box Hex value: 0x2E HeartBeat OpCode used when a client sends a heartbeat(keep-alive) ping Hex value: 0x45 HomepageRequest OpCode used when a client requests the url of the homepage Hex value: 0x68 Ignore OpCode used when a client ignores or un-ignores another player, or requests a list of ignored players Hex value: 0x0D ItemDrop OpCode used when a client drops an item on the ground Hex value: 0x08 ItemDroppedOnCreature OpCode used when a client drops an item on a creature Hex value: 0x29 ItemUse OpCode used when a client uses an item Hex value: 0x1A Login OpCode used when a client provides credentials to log into the world Hex value: 0x03 MapDataRequest OpCode used when a client requests tile data for the current map Hex value: 0x05 MenuInteraction OpCode used when a client responds to a merchant menu Hex value: 0x39 MetaDataRequest OpCode used when a client requests metadata details or data Hex value: 0x7B NoticeRequest OpCode used when a client requests EULA details Hex value: 0x4B OptionToggle OpCode used when a client toggles a user option Hex value: 0x1B PasswordChange OpCode used when a client requests to change a character's password Hex value: 0x26 Pickup OpCode used when a client picks up an item from the ground Hex value: 0x07 PublicMessage OpCode used when a client sends a publicly visible message Hex value: 0x0E RaiseStat OpCode used when a client requests to raise a stat Hex value: 0x47 RefreshRequest OpCode used when a client refreshes their viewport Hex value: 0x38 SelfProfileRequest OpCode used when a client requests their own profile Hex value: 0x2D SequenceChange OpCode used when a client requests to change the packet sequence number Hex value: 0x62 ServerTableRequest OpCode used when a client requests the details of available login servers Hex value: 0x57 SetNotepad OpCode used when a client sets persistent text on an object Hex value: 0x23 SkillUse OpCode used when a client uses a skill Hex value: 0x3E SocialStatus OpCode used when a client changes their social status Hex value: 0x79 Spacebar OpCode used when a client presses their spacebar Hex value: 0x13 SpellUse OpCode used when a client uses a spell Hex value: 0x0F SwapSlot OpCode used when a client swaps two panel objects Hex value: 0x30 SynchronizeTicks OpCode used when a client sends it's Environment.Ticks value Hex value: 0x75 ToggleGroup OpCode used when a client toggles their group availability Hex value: 0x2F Turn OpCode used when a client changes their character's direction Hex value: 0x11 Unequip OpCode used when a client unequips an item Hex value: 0x44 Version OpCode used when a client requests the encryption details, and a checksum of the details of available login servers Hex value: 0x00 Whisper OpCode used when a client sends a private message to the server Hex value: 0x19 WorldListRequest OpCode used when a client requests a list of all online players Hex value: 0x18 WorldMapClick OpCode used when a client clicks on a world map node Hex value: 0x3F Remarks In networking, an opcode is used to identify the type of packet that is being sent. The opcode is generally part of a header near the beginning of the data stream

api/Chaos.Networking.Abstractions.Defini.title: Enum ServerOpCode | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Networking.Abstractions.Defini.keywords: Enum ServerOpCode Namespace Chaos.Networking.Abstractions.Definitions Assembly Chaos.Networking.Abstractions.dll OpCodes used when sending packets to a client public enum ServerOpCode : byte Extension Methods ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T>(T, Action, Action<Exception>) ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T, TReturn>(T, Func<T, TReturn?>, Func<Exception, TReturn?>) Fields AcceptConnection OpCode sent to a client to confirm their initial connection Hex value: 0x7E AddItemToPane OpCode used to add an item to the client's inventory Hex value: 0x0F AddSkillToPane OpCode used to add a skill to a client's skillbook Hex value: 0x2C AddSpellToPane OpCode used to add a spell to the client's spellbook Hex value: 0x17 Animation OpCode used to play an animation on a point or object Hex value: 0x29 Attributes OpCode used to send a client it's attributes Hex value: 0x08 BodyAnimation OpCode used to animate a creature's body Hex value: 0x1A CancelCasting OpCode used to tell a client to cancel a spellcast Hex value: 0x48 ClientWalkResponse OpCode used to respond to a client's request to walk Hex value: 0x0B ConnectionInfo OpCode used to send the encryption details and checksum of the details of available login servers Hex value: 0x00 Cooldown OpCode used to send a skill or spell cooldown to a client Hex value: 0x3F CreatureTurn OpCode used to send a client another creature's turn Hex value: 0x11 CreatureWalk OpCode used to send a client another creature's walk Hex value: 0x0C DisplayAisling OpCode used to display an aisling to a client Hex value: 0x33 DisplayBoard OpCode used to display a board to a client Hex value: 0x31 DisplayDialog OpCode used to display a dialog to a client Hex value: 0x30 DisplayExchange OpCode used to send data displayed int an exchange window to a client Hex value: 0x42 DisplayGroupInvite OpCode used to send a client a group request Hex value: 0x63 DisplayMenu OpCode used to display a merchant menu to a client Hex value: 0x2F DisplayPublicMessage OpCode used to send a client a public message Hex value: 0x0D DisplayUnequip OpCode used to send a client a removal from an equipment slot Hex value: 0x38 DisplayVisibleEntities OpCode used to send a client all non-aisling objects in it's viewport Hex value: 0x07 Door OpCode used to give details of nearby doors to a client Hex value: 0x32 EditableProfileRequest OpCode used to request profile details from a client Hex value: 0x49 Effect OpCode used to display an effect on the bar on the right hand side of the viewport Hex value: 0x3A Equipment OpCode used to send a client a change in an equipment slot Hex value: 0x37 ExitResponse OpCode used to send a client confirmation of a request to exit to the login server Hex value: 0x4C ForceClientPacket OpCode used to force a client to send back a specified packet Hex value: 0x4B HealthBar OpCode used to display a creature's health bar Hex value: 0x13 HeartBeatResponse OpCode used to respond to a client's heartbeat Hex value: 0x3B LightLevel OpCode used to change the light level of the map Hex value: 0x20 Location OpCode used to send a client it's location Hex value: 0x04 LoginControl OpCode used to send a client data for the login screen Hex value: 0x66 LoginMessage OpCode used to send a message to a client on the login server Hex value: 0x02 LoginNotice OpCode used to send a client the EULA / login notice Hex value: 0x60 MapChangeComplete OpCode used to signal to the client that a map change operation has completed Hex value: 0x1F MapChangePending OpCode used to signal a client that it is changing maps Hex value: 0x67 MapData OpCode used to send a client tile data for a map Hex value: 0x3C MapInfo OpCode used to send map details to a client Hex value: 0x15 MapLoadComplete OpCode used to signal a client that it has finished sending map data Hex value: 0x58 MetaData OpCode used to send metadata data to a client Hex value: 0x6F Notepad OpCode used to associate text with an object Hex value: 0x1B OtherProfile OpCode used to display an aisling's profile to a client Hex value: 0x34 Redirect OpCode used to redirect a client to another server Hex value: 0x03 RefreshResponse OpCode used to respond to a client's request to refresh the viewport Hex value: 0x22 RemoveEntity OpCode used to remove an object from the client's viewport Hex value: 0x0E RemoveItemFromPane OpCode used to remove an item from the client's inventory Hex value: 0x10 RemoveSkillFromPane OpCode used to remove a skill from a client's skillbook Hex value: 0x2D RemoveSpellFromPane OpCode used to remove a spell from the client's spellbook Hex value: 0x18 SelfProfile OpCode used to send a client it's own profile Hex value: 0x39 ServerMessage OpCode used to send a client a non-public message Hex value: 0x0A ServerTableResponse OpCode used to send a client a list of available servers Hex value: 0x56 Sound OpCode used to tell the client to play a sound Hex value: 0x19 SynchronizeTicksResponse OpCode used to synchronize the server's ticks with a client's Hex value: 0x68 UserId OpCode used to send a client it's id Hex value: 0x05 WorldList OpCode used to display the world list to a client Hex value: 0x36 WorldMap OpCode used to transition a client to the world map Hex value: 0x2E Remarks In networking, an opcode is used to identify the type of packet that is being sent. The opcode is generally part of a header near the beginning of the data stream

SendDisplayDialog(DisplayDialogArgs) Sends a packet to display a dialog SendDisplayExchange(DisplayExchangeArgs) Sends a packet to display, close, or update an exchange SendDisplayGroupInvite(DisplayGroupInviteArgs) Sends a packet to display a group invite SendDisplayPublicMessage(DisplayPublicMessageArgs) Sends a packet to display a public message. (Normal, Shout, Chant) SendDisplayUnequip(DisplayUnequipArgs) Sends a packet to unequip an item (remove from equipment pane) SendDisplayVisibleEntities(DisplayVisibleEntitiesArgs) Sends a packet to display visible entities to the client (Monsters, Merchants, GroundItems only) SendDoors(DoorArgs) Sends a packet to display doors SendEditableProfileRequest() Sends a packet to request the editable profile from the client. (Profile text and image) SendEffect(EffectArgs) Sends a packet to display an effect in the effect bar (top right of screen) SendEquipment(EquipmentArgs) Sends a packet to display an item in the equipment pane SendExitResponse(ExitResponseArgs) Sends a packet to respond to a client's exit request SendForceClientPacket(ForceClientPacketArgs) Sends a packet to force a client to send a packet to the server SendHealthBar(HealthBarArgs) Sends a packet to display a health bar SendLightLevel(LightLevelArgs) Sends a packet to update the light level of the client (day/night) SendLocation(LocationArgs) Sends a packet to update the client's location SendMapChangeComplete() Sends a packet to signal the client that a map change is complete SendMapChangePending() Sends a packet to signal the client that a map change is pending SendMapData(MapDataArgs) Sends a packet to send map data to the client SendMapInfo(MapInfoArgs) Sends a packet to send map information to the client SendMapLoadComplete() Sends a packet to signal the client that the map has finished loading SendMetaData(MetaDataArgs) Sends meta data to the client SendNotepad(NotepadArgs) Sends a packet to display an editable notepad SendOtherProfile(OtherProfileArgs) Sends a packet to display another player's profile SendRefreshResponse() Sends a packet to respond to a request to refresh the client. This is more for signalling that the refresh operation has completed. SendRemoveEntity(RemoveEntityArgs) Sends a packet to remove an entity from the client SendRemoveItemFromPane(RemoveItemFromPaneArgs) Sends a packet to remove an item from a pane SendRemoveSkillFromPane(RemoveSkillFromPaneArgs) Sends a packet to remove a skill from a pane SendRemoveSpellFromPane(RemoveSpellFromPaneArgs) Sends a packet to remove a spell from a pane SendSelfProfile(SelfProfileArgs) Sends a packet containing the client's profile SendServerMessage(ServerMessageArgs) Sends a packet to display a server message (Whisper, Guild, Group, OrangeBar, WhiteText, etc) SendSound(SoundArgs) Sends a packet to play a sound (or music) SendUserId(UserIdArgs) Sends a packet to give the client it's user id. This is what the client uses to attach it's viewport to an aisling. SendWorldList(WorldListArgs) Sends a packet to display a world list to the client SendWorldMap(WorldMapArgs) Sends a packet to display a world map to the client

api/Chaos.Networking.Abstractions.html.title: Namespace Chaos.Networking.Abstractions | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Networking.Abstractions.html.keywords: Namespace Chaos.Networking.Abstractions Classes ConnectedClientBase Represents a client that is connected to an IServer<TClient>. This class defines the methods used to communicate with the client. LobbyClientBase Represents a client connected to a lobby server. LoginClientBase Represents a client connected to a login server. ServerBase<T> Represents a base class for server implementations. SocketClientBase Provides the ability to send and receive packets over a socket WorldClientBase Represents a client connected to the world server Interfaces IChaosLobbyClient IChaosLoginClient IChaosWorldClient IClient Defines a pattern for a client that connects to an IServer<TClient> IClientRegistry<T> Defines the pattern for a service that clients register to so that they are more easily discovered IConnectedClient Defines a pattern for an object that represents a client connected to an IServer<TClient>. This interface contains the definitions used to communicate with the client IConnectionInfo Defines a configuration object thats contains connection information for a server ILobbyClient Defines a pattern for an object that represents a client connected to a lobby server. ILobbyServer<TClient> Defines a pattern for server that presents a list of available login servers to connect to ILoginClient Represents a client that is connected to the login server. ILoginServerInfo Defines a configuration object that is used to describe a login server ILoginServer<TClient> Defines a pattern for a server that facilitates character creation, presentation of the EULA, and the ability to log into a character or change a character's password INotice Represents the notice shown when logging into a server IRedirect Represents an object used to instruct and track a client during a redirect operation IRedirectManager Defines the properties and methods for managing redirects IServerTable Defines the requirements for a server table. The server table is used to display available servers to connect to from the lobby server. IServer<TClient> Defines the bare minimum for a server ISocketClient Defines a pattern for an object that has the ability to send and receive packets over a socket connection IWorldClient Represents a client that is connected to the world server. IWorldServer<TClient> Defines a pattern for a server that facilites all aspects of actual gameplay Delegates ServerBase<T>.ClientHandler Delegate for handling client packets.

api/Chaos.Networking.Entities.Server.Dis.keywords: Property RightSide Namespace Chaos.Networking.Entities.Server Assembly Chaos.Networking.dll RightSide Whether or not this packet is interacting with the right side of the exchange window.  Bink

* {LeaderName}" --- Otherwise: "
 {MemberName}" - Ends with "Total {Count}"

api/Chaos.Schemas.Content.MapInstanceSch.title: Class MapInstanceSchema | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Schemas.Content.MapInstanceSch.keywords: Class MapInstanceSchema Namespace Chaos.Schemas.Content Assembly Chaos.Schemas.dll Represents the serializable schema of a map instance public sealed record MapInstanceSchema : IEquatable<MapInstanceSchema> Inheritance object MapInstanceSchema Implements IEquatable<MapInstanceSchema> Extension Methods ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T>(T, Action, Action<Exception>) ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T, TReturn>(T, Func<T, TReturn?>, Func<Exception, TReturn?>) Properties AutoDayNightCycle Whether the map will experience day/night cycles automatically Flags A flag, or combination of flags that should affect the map You can combine multiple flags by separating them with commas Ex. "Snow, NoTabMap" InstanceId A unique id specific to this map instance This must match the name of the folder containing this file MaximumLevel Default null If specified, sets the minimum level needed to enter this map via warp tile MinimumLevel Default null If specified, sets the maximum level allowed to enter this map via warp tile Music The byte values of the music track to play when entering the map These values aren't explored yet, so you'll have to figure out what's available yourself Name The name of the map that will display in-game ScriptKeys A collection of script keys for scripts to load for this map (TODO: scripts section) ShardingOptions Default null If specified, these options will be used to determine how this instance will shard itself TemplateKey A string representation of the map id. Ex. 500 for mileth

api/Chaos.Schemas.Data.ShardingOptionsSc.title: Property ExitLocation | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Schemas.Data.ShardingOptionsSc.keywords: Property ExitLocation Namespace Chaos.Schemas.Data Assembly Chaos.Schemas.dll ExitLocation The instanceId to teleport players to if they log on and the instance no longer exists public Location ExitLocation { get; set; } Property Value Location Remarks Imagine a scenario where a players disconnects. They should be able to log back into the instance they were already in. Now imagine a scenario where a player logs off, then logs back on 5 hours later. The instance will likely no longer exist. We can't simply create a new instance for them, this would be abusable (bosses would respawn, events would reset, etc) This instanceid is the place to put people who are in this scenario, generally speaking it should be the map you enter this instance from

api/Chaos.Scripting.DialogScripts.Abstra.keywords: Method OnNext Namespace Chaos.Scripting.DialogScripts.Abstractions Assembly Chaos.dll OnNext(Aisling, byte?) Called when the player responds to the dialog, whether by clicking Next, selecting an option, or making any kind of selection If an option is clicked, the optionIndex will be the index of the option that was clicked If a shop selection was clicked, the text of that option will be in the MenuArgs property of the dialog If a selection was made from the player's inventory, spellbook, or skillbook, the Slot that was selected will be in the MenuArgs property of the dialog public virtual void OnNext(Aisling source, byte? optionIndex = null) Parameters source Aisling The aisling the dialog is for optionIndex byte? The index of the option that was selected, if any

api/Chaos.Scripting.DialogScripts.GuildS.title: Method OnNext | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Scripting.DialogScripts.GuildS.keywords: Method OnNext Namespace Chaos.Scripting.DialogScripts.GuildScripts Assembly Chaos.dll OnNext(Aisling, byte?) Called when the player responds to the dialog, whether by clicking Next, selecting an option, or making any kind of selection If an option is clicked, the optionIndex will be the index of the option that was clicked If a shop selection was clicked, the text of that option will be in the MenuArgs property of the dialog If a selection was made from the player's inventory, spellbook, or skillbook, the Slot that was selected will be in the MenuArgs property of the dialog public override void OnNext(Aisling source, byte? optionIndex = null) Parameters source Aisling The aisling the dialog is for optionIndex byte? The index of the option that was selected, if any

api/Chaos.Scripting.DialogScripts.Telepo.title: Method OnNext | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Scripting.DialogScripts.Telepo.keywords: Method OnNext Namespace Chaos.Scripting.DialogScripts Assembly Chaos.dll OnNext(Aisling, byte?) Called when the player responds to the dialog, whether by clicking Next, selecting an option, or making any kind of selection If an option is clicked, the optionIndex will be the index of the option that was clicked If a shop selection was clicked, the text of that option will be in the MenuArgs property of the dialog If a selection was made from the player's inventory, spellbook, or skillbook, the Slot that was selected will be in the MenuArgs property of the dialog public override void OnNext(Aisling source, byte? optionIndex = null) Parameters source Aisling The aisling the dialog is for optionIndex byte? The index of the option that was selected, if any

api/Chaos.Scripting.ScriptFactory-2.html.keywords: Class ScriptFactory<TScript, TScripted> Namespace Chaos.Scripting Assembly Chaos.Scripting.dll A factory object that generates IScripts public sealed class ScriptFactory<TScript, TScripted> : IScriptFactory<TScript, TScripted> where TScript : IScript where TScripted : IScripted Type Parameters TScript A type of script TScripted A type of scripted object Inheritance object ScriptFactory<TScript, TScripted> Implements IScriptFactory<TScript, TScripted> Extension Methods ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T>(T, Action, Action<Exception>) ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T, TReturn>(T, Func<T, TReturn?>, Func<Exception, TReturn?>) Remarks This object requires that any given IScript type has an implemented ICompositeScript<TScript> type. This script factory will utilize that composite script to compose multiple scripts into one. The script returned by this factory will always be the ICompositeScript<TScript> implementation, and it will contain all of scripts generated from the keys that are supplied. api/Chaos.Security.Abstractions.Credenti.keywords: Enum CredentialValidationResult.FailureCode Namespace Chaos.Security.Abstractions Assembly Chaos.Security.Abstractions.dll The code that represents the result of the validation public enum CredentialValidationResult.FailureCode Extension Methods ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T>(T, Action, Action<Exception>) ObjectExtensions.TryCatch<T, TReturn>(T, Func<T, TReturn?>, Func<Exception, TReturn?>) Fields InvalidPassword = 2 The validation failed because the provided password was invalid InvalidUsername = 1 The validation failed because the provided username was invalid PasswordTooLong = 3 The validation failed because the provided password was too long PasswordTooShort = 4 The validation failed because the provided password was too short Success = 0 The validation succeeded TooManyAttempts = 8 The validation failed because the ip has failed validation too many times UsernameNotAllowed = 7 The validation failed because the provided username was not allowed UsernameTooLong = 5 The validation failed because the provided username was too long UsernameTooShort = 6 The validation failed because the provided username was too short

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.title: Property AislingAssailIntervalMs | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.keywords: Property AislingAssailIntervalMs Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll AislingAssailIntervalMs All creatures have an AssailInterval. AssailInterval is essentially the cooldown in milliseconds for skills marked as assails. This value ismodified by the AtkSpeedPct attribute and can be modified to be 3x faster or slower than the base value. This property sets the base interval for all aislings. A good starting value is 1500 int AislingAssailIntervalMs { get; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.title: Property MaxActionsPerSecond | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.keywords: Property MaxActionsPerSecond Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll MaxActionsPerSecond It would be bad to allow players to perform an infinite number of actions per second. Anything without a cooldown could become a huge burden on the server. This value is used to control the maximum number of actions a player can take in a second. An action is defined as using any spell, skill, or item. This includes equipping items. A good range of values for this would be 4 - 10 If desired, this value can be set to 3 to emulate the original game int MaxActionsPerSecond { get; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.title: Property MaxChantTimeBurdenMs | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.keywords: Property MaxChantTimeBurdenMs Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll MaxChantTimeBurdenMs When players cast spells, each spell line takes approximately 1000ms to chant. The amount of time a spell will take to cast can be predicted to be 1000ms * (NumSpellLines). Due to latency and jitter, players will often cast spells for slightly more or less than the expected amount of time. With big latency spikes, the observed amount of time could be far off than the expected value. To be able to tolerate this while also prohibiting "speed casting", the server will allow spell casts that occur too quickly and add up a time burden. Each time a player casts a spell that completes faster than expected, the difference in time will be added to the time burden. This burden will accumulate with every consecutive spell that occurs too quickly, and be subtracted from if a spell completes too slowly. The time burden will also decrease while not casting spells. If the time burden exceeds MaxChantTimeBurdenMs, the spell cast will be ignored. A good range of values for this setting would be 500 - 1500, with lower values being more strict. int MaxChantTimeBurdenMs { get; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.keywords: Property MaxGoldHeld Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll MaxGoldHeld This is the maximum amount of gold a player can hold in their inventory. int MaxGoldHeld { get; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.title: Property MinimumAislingAc | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.keywords: Property MinimumAislingAc Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll MinimumAislingAc This is the minimum amount of AC a player can have. damage formulas can be changed, but with the default damage formula, lower AC = less damage taken. api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.keywords: Property MinimumMonsterAc Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll MinimumMonsterAc This is the minimum amount of AC a monster can have. damage formulas can be changed, but with the default damage formula, lower AC = less damage taken. api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.title: Property PickupRange | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.keywords: Property PickupRange Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll PickupRange This is the maximum distance from a player that they pick up items or money from the ground. A value of -1 would effectively disable picking up items A value of 0 would only allow players to pick up items from directly beneath them A value of 12 would allow players to pick up items from anywhere in their viewport int PickupRange { get; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.title: Property ProhibitF5Walk | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.keywords: Property ProhibitF5Walk Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll ProhibitF5Walk When this is enabled it will prevent players from utilizing refreshing(F5) to walk faster. bool ProhibitF5Walk { get; } Property Value bool

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.title: Property ProhibitItemSwitchWalk | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.keywords: Property ProhibitItemSwitchWalk Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll ProhibitItemSwitchWalk When this is enabled it will prevent players from utilizing item switching to walk faster. bool ProhibitItemSwitchWalk { get; } Property Value bool

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.title: Property ProhibitSpeedWalk | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.keywords: Property ProhibitSpeedWalk Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll ProhibitSpeedWalk When this is enabled it will prevent players from utilizing more nefarious methods to walk faster. bool ProhibitSpeedWalk { get; } Property Value bool

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.title: Property RefreshIntervalMs | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.keywords: Property RefreshIntervalMs Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll RefreshIntervalMs This is the quickest interval in milliseconds that players will be allowed to refresh their client. This will not include refreshes utilized by the server, such as for refreshing a player's position if they walk into a wall. A good value here would be 1000 int RefreshIntervalMs { get; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.title: Property SaveIntervalMins | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.keywords: Property SaveIntervalMins Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll SaveIntervalMins This is the amount of time in minutes between global character saves. This is on top of players automatically saving when they log out. Do not set this value too low or it will become a burden on the server. A good range of values would be anywhere from 0.5 - 10 double SaveIntervalMins { get; } Property Value double

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.title: Property SleepAnimationTimerMins | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.keywords: Property SleepAnimationTimerMins Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll SleepAnimationTimerMins This is the amount of time in minutes after a player's last action where the server will play the sleep animation int SleepAnimationTimerMins { get; set; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.title: Property TradeRange | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.keywords: Property TradeRange Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll TradeRange This is the maximum distance from a player that they can initiate a trade with another player A value of -1 would effectively disable trading A value of 0 would only allow players to only trade with players on the same tile, which is generally impossible A value of 12 would allow players to trade with anyone in their viewport int TradeRange { get; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.title: Property UpdatesPerSecond | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.IWorl.keywords: Property UpdatesPerSecond Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll UpdatesPerSecond This is the number of times per second that the server will update the game state. .NET isn't great for this kind of workload due garbage collection and JIT recompilation/OSR. I suggest a hard cap of 60 with a good default value of 30 If desired you can set this to 3 to emulate the original game int UpdatesPerSecond { get; } Property Value int

A good starting value is 1500 public required int AislingAssailIntervalMs { get; init; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.title: Property MaxActionsPerSecond | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.keywords: Property MaxActionsPerSecond Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll MaxActionsPerSecond It would be bad to allow players to perform an infinite number of actions per second. Anything without a cooldown could become a huge burden on the server. This value is used to control the maximum number of actions a player can take in a second. An action is defined as using any spell, skill, or item. This includes equipping items. A good range of values for this would be 4 - 10 If desired, this value can be set to 3 to emulate the original game public required int MaxActionsPerSecond { get; init; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.title: Property MaxChantTimeBurdenMs | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.keywords: Property MaxChantTimeBurdenMs Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll MaxChantTimeBurdenMs When players cast spells, each spell line takes approximately 1000ms to chant. The amount of time a spell will take to cast can be predicted to be 1000ms * (NumSpellLines). Due to latency and jitter, players will often cast spells for slightly more or less than the expected amount of time. With big latency spikes, the observed amount of time could be far off than the expected value. To be able to tolerate this while also prohibiting "speed casting", the server will allow spell casts that occur too quickly and add up a time burden. Each time a player casts a spell that completes faster than expected, the difference in time will be added to the time burden. This burden will accumulate with every consecutive spell that occurs too quickly, and be subtracted from if a spell completes too slowly. The time burden will also decrease while not casting spells. If the time burden exceeds MaxChantTimeBurdenMs, the spell cast will be ignored. A good range of values for this setting would be 500 - 1500, with lower values being more strict. public required int MaxChantTimeBurdenMs { get; init; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.title: Property MaxGoldHeld | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.keywords: Property MaxGoldHeld Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll MaxGoldHeld This is the maximum amount of gold a player can hold in their inventory. public required int MaxGoldHeld { get; init; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.keywords: Property MaxGroupSize Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll MaxGroupSize The maximum number of players that can be in a group together. If a group reaches this size, invites and invite accepts will fail. public required int MaxGroupSize { get; init; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.title: Property MaxLevel | Chaos Server Docs

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.title: Property MaximumMonsterAc | Chaos Server Docs

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.title: Property MinimumAislingAc | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.keywords: Property MinimumAislingAc Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll MinimumAislingAc This is the minimum amount of AC a player can have. damage formulas can be changed, but with the default damage formula, lower AC = less damage taken. With the default damage formula, AC is a percentile, so -100 AC would make you invulnerable. public required int MinimumAislingAc { get; init; } Property Value int

A value of -1 would effectively disable picking up items A value of 0 would only allow players to pick up items from directly beneath them A value of 12 would allow players to pick up items from anywhere in their viewport public required int PickupRange { get; init; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.keywords: Property ProhibitItemSwitchWalk Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll ProhibitItemSwitchWalk When this is enabled it will prevent players from utilizing item switching to walk faster. public required bool ProhibitItemSwitchWalk { get; init; } Property Value bool

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.title: Property ProhibitSpeedWalk | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.keywords: Property ProhibitSpeedWalk Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll ProhibitSpeedWalk When this is enabled it will prevent players from utilizing more nefarious methods to walk faster. public required bool ProhibitSpeedWalk { get; init; } Property Value bool

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.title: Property RefreshIntervalMs | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.keywords: Property RefreshIntervalMs Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll RefreshIntervalMs This is the quickest interval in milliseconds that players will be allowed to refresh their client. This will not include refreshes utilized by the server, such as for refreshing a player's position if they walk into a wall. A good value here would be 1000 public required int RefreshIntervalMs { get; init; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.title: Property SaveIntervalMins | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.keywords: Property SaveIntervalMins Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll SaveIntervalMins This is the amount of time in minutes between global character saves. This is on top of players automatically saving when they log out. Do not set this value too low or it will become a burden on the server. A good range of values would be anywhere from 0.5 - 10 public required double SaveIntervalMins { get; init; } Property Value double

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.title: Property SleepAnimationTimerMins | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.keywords: Property SleepAnimationTimerMins Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll SleepAnimationTimerMins This is the amount of time in minutes after a player's last action where the server will play the sleep animation public int SleepAnimationTimerMins { get; set; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.title: Property TradeRange | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.keywords: Property TradeRange Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll TradeRange This is the maximum distance from a player that they can initiate a trade with another player A value of -1 would effectively disable trading A value of 0 would only allow players to only trade with players on the same tile, which is generally impossible A value of 12 would allow players to trade with anyone in their viewport public required int TradeRange { get; init; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.title: Property UpdatesPerSecond | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Services.Servers.Options.World.keywords: Property UpdatesPerSecond Namespace Chaos.Services.Servers.Options Assembly Chaos.dll UpdatesPerSecond This is the number of times per second that the server will update the game state. .NET isn't great for this kind of workload due garbage collection and JIT recompilation/OSR. I suggest a hard cap of 60 with a good default value of 30 If desired you can set this to 3 to emulate the original game public required int UpdatesPerSecond { get; init; } Property Value int

api/Chaos.Time.html.title: Namespace Chaos.Time | Chaos Server Docs
api/Chaos.Time.html.keywords: Namespace Chaos.Time Classes ChantTimer Keeps track of when spell chants start and end, and validates whether or not a spell should be allowed to finish casting. DeltaMonitor Monitors the execution time of a tight loop. Logs output so the used has better insight into how long execution is taking. DeltaTime Provides an easy way to obtain a high-precision time-based delta value. IntervalTimer A timer that utilizes a delta time value to increment elapsed time PeriodicMessageTimer PeriodicSequentialEventTimer A timer that runs a sequence of timers in order. When one timer has elapsed, the next timer begins elapsing. This timer is periodic, meaning it will always update the first timer in the sequence, even if other timers are elapsing. RandomizedIntervalTimer A timers that utilizes a delta time value to increment elapsed time. Each time the interval elapses, a new interval is set with an amount of randomization as specified through configuration RegenTimer ResettingCounter A counter that resets each time a specified time period has elapsed, utilizing a delta time value SequentialEventTimer A timer that runs a sequence of timers in order. When one timer has elapsed, the next timer begins elapsing. When the last timer has elapsed, the first timer begins again. Structs GameTime A System.DateTime replacement that runs at 24x speed, to be used for in-game time measurement

articles/AccessManager.html.title: Access Manager | Chaos Server Docs
articles/AccessManager.html.keywords: Access Manager The access manager is exactly what it sounds like, but also more. This is either a poorly named class, or an object that has too many jobs. Maybe I'll remedy this in the future, but for now it is what it is. It handles not just access, but also player username and password rules and other various rules for character creation. Configuration The access manager is configured via the Options:AccessManagerOptions section of the appsettings.json file. These options are serialized into AccessManagerOptions Type Name Description string Directory The relative directory where the Access Manager will store its files string HashAlgorithmName The name of the hash algorithm used to hash passwords string LockoutMins The number of minutes an IP is locked out for after failing to login too many times string MaxCredentialAttempts The maximum number of times an IP can fail to login or change password before being locked out for a period of time string MaxPasswordLength The maximum number of characters allowed in a password string MaxUsernameLength The maximum number of characters allowed in a username string MinPasswordLength The minimum number of characters allowed in a password string MinUsernameLength The minimum number of characters allowed in a username string Mode The mode in which that the access manager operates string PhraseFilter A list of phrases/words that a username can not contains string ReservedUsernames A list of usernames that are reserved and can not be used string ValidCharactersPattern A regular expression used to validate that a username only contains valid characters string ValidFormatPattern A regular expression used to validate that a username is in the correct format Note I suggest using https://regex101.com/ to test your regular expressions Example appsettings.json "AccessManagerOptions": { "Directory": "Data\\Configuration\\Access", "ValidCharactersPattern": "[a-zA-Z0-9 ]+", "ValidFormatPattern": "^[a-zA-Z]{3,}$|[a-zA-Z]{3,} ?[a-zA-Z]{3,}", "MaxUsernameLength": 12, "MinUsernameLength": 3, "MaxPasswordLength": 8, "MinPasswordLength": 5, "ReservedUsernames": [], "PhraseFilter": [], "HashAlgorithmName": "SHA512", "Mode": "Blacklist", "MaxCredentialAttempts": 5, "LockoutMins": 5 }, Mode The mode can be either Whitelist, or Blacklist. In whitelist mode, only the IPs in the whitelist file are allowed to connect. In blacklist mode, all IPs are allowed to connect, except those in the blacklist file. These files are located in the directory specified and the contents of these files should be IP addresses. They can be IPv4 or IPv6 addresses. If the file contains a line that is not a valid IP, it will be ignored.

articles/BulletinBoards.html.title: Bulletin Boards | Chaos Server Docs
articles/BulletinBoards.html.keywords: Bulletin Boards Boards in Chaos are both templated and scripted objects. When you define a board, you are defining the read-only configuration of that board across the game. BulletinBoards are created from that configuration, with it's posts being separately serialized and deserialized elsewhere. BulletinBoard Templates A bulletin board template is a configuration object that defines the read-only configuration of a bulletin board. How do I create them? By default, bulletin board templates should be created in Data\Configuration\Tempaltes\BulletinBoards. Configuration of how bulletin boards are loaded can be found in appsettings.json at Options:BulletinBoardTemplateCacheOptions. BulletinBoard templates are initially serialized into BulletinBoardTemplateSchema before being mapped to a BulletinBoardTemplate. The schema object is mapped via the BoardMapperProfile. See BulletinBoardTemplateSchema for a list of all configurable properties with descriptions. How do I use them? BulletinBoards are loaded via the BulletinBoardStore, which is an implementation of IStore<T>. Note Objects stored via IStore<T> are loaded on-demand, and never go out of scope private readonly IStore<BulletinBoard> BulletinBoardStore; public Foo(IStore<BulletinBoard> bulletinBoardStore) => BulletinBoardStore = bulletinBoardStore; public void Bar() { //this will fetch the bulletin board that has the key specified var bulletinBoard = BulletinBoardStore.Load("myBoardKey"); } MailBoxes MailBoxes are handled slightly differently, and are serialized to and from MailBoxSchema. MailBoxes are not configurable or scripted, and are created when an Aisling is created. Configuration of how MailBoxes are saved and backed up can be found in appsettings.json at Options:MailBoxStoreOptions. How do I use them? MailBoxes are made accessible through the MailStore, which is an implementation of IStore<T>. Note Objects stored via IStore<T> are loaded on-demand, and never go out of scope private readonly IStore<MailBox> MailStore; public Foo(IStore<MailBox> mailStore) => MailStore = mailStore; public void Bar() { //this will fetch the mailbox for the specified aisling name var mailBox = MailStore.Load("sichi"); } Board List When logged onto a character, there's a button you can press to see a list of boards that you have access to. This list of boards is configurable through the DefaultAislingScript. You can also display a list of boards via other scripts, such as a reactor tile, by calling private readonly IStore<MailBox> MailStore; private readonly IStore<BulletinBoard> BulletinBoardStore; public Foo(IStore<MailBox> mailStore, IStore<BulletinBoard> bulletinBoardStore) { MailStore = mailStore; BulletinBoardStore = bulletinBoardStore; } public void Bar(IWorldClient client) { //fetch mailbox var mailBox = MailStore.Load("sichi"); //fetch other board var bulletinBoard = BulletinBoardStore.Load("myBoardKey"); //display both boards to the client client.SendBoardList(new[] { mailBox, bulletinBoard }); } BulletinBoards in the world If you want to display a board by clicking a tile on the map, you can use a reactor tile. Just place a reactor tile on the point the tile is at, and add a script to that reactor tile to display the board when clicked. Scripting BulletinBoards are scripted via IBulletinBoardScript. Inherit from BulletinBoardScriptBase for a basic script that requires no external configuration Inherit from ConfigurableBulletinBoardScriptBase for a script that requires external configuration via ScriptVars Specify any number of script keys in the BulletinBoardTemplate.ScriptKeys property, and those scripts will automatically be attached to the BulletinBoard when it is created. If the script is configurable, you must also have an entry for that script in the BulletinBoardTemplate.ScriptVars property. Note The key of a script is the name of the class without 'Script' at the end Here are the events overridable in bulletinBoard scripts Event Name Context AllowedToDelete Called when an aisling is trying to delete a post AllowedToHighlight Called when an aisling is trying to highlight a post. It's also used to determine if an aisling has the ability to see the highlight button in the UI AllowedToPost Called when an aisling is trying to post to the board AllowedToView Called when an aisling is trying to view the board, or a post on the board ShouldRejectPost Called when an aisling is trying to post to the board. This event should be used to filter out posts for whatever reason you decide Update Called every time boards update, which is about once per minute. Boards update separately from all other objects. Only boards that have been loaded will be updated See Scripting for more details on scripting in general Example Here is an example of a public bulletin board that is configured to use the "publicBoard" script, which retains posts indefinitely, and is moderated by a character named "fooBar". (more on scripts later) { "templateKey": "public_test_board", "name": "Test Board", "scriptKeys": ["publicBoard"], "scriptVars": { "publicBoard": { "postRetentionHours": -1, "moderators": ["fooBar"] } } }

articles/CachingAndSerialization.html.title: Caching | Chaos Server Docs
articles/CachingAndSerialization.html.keywords: Caching Chaos starts up with nothing loaded. When something is requested, be it a SkillTemplate, ItemTemplate, or even a MapInstance, it is fetched and cached for a period of time. If that cached object is not accessed again within that period of time, it is removed from the cache. This is done to significantly reduce the memory and cpu usage of the server. Chaos utilizes the ISimpleCache interface. Default implementations of this interface use microsoft's IMemoryCache. Most of the caches that Chaos uses are customizable via appsettings.json. See ExpiringFileCacheOptions for more information. Schema Objects These default caching implementations rely on Chaos.Schemas objects as an intermediary for serialization. Think of these schema objects as DTOs(Data Transfer Objects). They are intended to be bags of properties with no functionality. They exist only to be serialized or deserialized. Object mapping In many cases, the schema objects are not the same as the actual objects that Chaos uses for interactions. In most scenarios, it doesn't make a lot of sense to serialize every detail of an object. Many of the basic properties of an item are already saved via a template that you have created. That template serves as the building block of that object. In cases like this, we really only need to serialize a way to unique identify that template. This is where object mapping comes in. Chaos utilizes IMapperProfiles to map between the schema objects and the actual objects. Profiles can utilize other services freely, and if nested mapping is needed they can access each other through the ITypeMapper service. Note By default, all profiles in the solution are loaded into the IoC container at startup by the Mapper service. Serialization By default, Chaos uses System.Text.Json for serialization ( See SerializationContext). If there is a need to use some other medium of storage and serialization, you can can create your own ISimpleCache implementations that do not serialize from json.

articles/ChaosAssetManager.html.title: ChaosAssetManager | Chaos Server Docs
articles/ChaosAssetManager.html.keywords: ChaosAssetManager ChaosAssetManager is a tool built around dalib that lets you interact with the dat files. It also contains several useful editors for manipulating images, metadata, and more. If the functionality you need is not present, feel free to make a request. Alternatively, you could use dalib directly. Dalib has far more functionality than what is presented in this application. Usage Clone the ChaosAssetManager repo Also clone the dalib They must be cloned into the same directory, like so: 📂sichii ┣📂ChaosAssetManager ┃ ┗📄ChaosAssetManager.sln ┗📂dalib ┗📄dalib.sln Alternatively, clone them wherever you want and fix the references in the ChaosAssetManager project. Features view, patch, compile, decompile all dat files convert to/from any dat file format (except .hea) effect editor for for .EFA and .EPF files (allows you to center the effects as they would be displayed in game) metafile editor palette remapper Future Features mpf creator map editor maybe anything else creators request that might be generally useful

articles/ChaosTool.html.title: ChaosTool | Chaos Server Docs
articles/ChaosTool.html.keywords: ChaosTool ChaosTool is a tool to help create content for Chaos-Server. It is a WPF application that has that ability to load all json files using settings from Chaos and allows you to edit all properties except ScriptVars. Usage ChaosTool is included in the project under Tools/ChaosTool. It will read configuration from your existing appsettings.json files to find where your data is stored. Features load and display items, spells, skills, and dialogs add, update, delete for each entity type add, update, delete all properties except ScriptVars bulk editing of all entity types via BulkEdit tab (see below) revert button allows you to undo changes as long as you haven't saved an editable form view for each entity type start typing into the left-hand side list to search there specifically CTRL+F functionality to search otherwise many kinds of integrity checks for all entity types BulkEdit The BulkEdit tab allows you to write a script in the tool, giving you access to everything in the JsonContext. To add, update, or delete entities in bulk, just access JsonContext. At the end of your script, make sure to call await JsonContext.SaveChangesAsync(); Future Features rendered panel sprites (item, skill, spell) rendered creature sprites map viewer

articles/Commands.html.title: Commands | Chaos Server Docs
articles/Commands.html.keywords: Commands Commands are a way to execute some code within the game by using the chat window. Commands have some configuration that can be changed in the appsettings.json file. The prefix can be changed via Options:AislingCommandInterceptorOptions:Prefix New commands are created by inheriting from the ICommand interface, and adding the CommandAttribute attribute to the class. In the attribute is specified the command name that will be used to execute the command, and whether or not the command requires admin privileges. Chaos comes with many commands out of the box, and you can find them in the Chaos.Messaging namespace. Note Admin privileges are granted by adding the "IsAdmin": true to an aisling's json file Execution Commands are intercepted by looking for the configured prefix, followed by a command's name. Commands are created newly for each execution, and are dependency injected. Arguments Commands are provided the object that executed the command (in this case, an Aisling), and an ArgumentCollection. The ArgumentCollection helps to facilitate conversion of strings to other data types, as well as usage of familiar patterns in command line interfaces, such as preserving arguments that have spaces within double quotes(""). The ArgumentCollection can be accessed by index via the TryGet method, or by accessing arguments linearly via the TryGetNext method. Note TryGetNext will not increment the index if it returns false

articles/Components.html.title: Components | Chaos Server Docs
articles/Components.html.keywords: Components Components are a way of separating functionality into smaller, more manageable pieces. They let you pull pieces of logic out of scripts so that you can reuse them in multiple places and multiple different script types. For example, both skills and spells can have animations, body animations, sounds, deal damage, and more. Each of these things can be separated into their own components. Component types There are 2 kinds of components: IComponent and IConditionalComponent. IComponent is used to execute a piece of logic when nothing depends on its execution. For example, animation or sound. There is no way for these actions to "fail", and thus nothing is going to depend on those components' execution. Most components will be of this type. public class MyComponent : IComponent { public void Execute(ActivationContext context, ComponentVars vars) { // Do something } } IConditionalComponent is used when something depends on the execution of the component. For example, a component that makes an ability occasionally fail, or a component to make abilities cost mana. If components like these fail, the rest of the components that come after it should not execute. public class MyConditionalComponent : IConditionalComponent { public bool Execute(ActivationContext context, ComponentVars vars) { // Do something // Return true if the component succeeded, false if it failed return true; } } Component options Any component that has options associated with it should create an interface that specifies those options. The component should expect its options to be contained within the ComponentVars passed to the Execute method of the component. The options can be obtained via ComponentVars.GetOptions<TOptions>(). public class MyComponent : IComponent { public interface IMyComponentOptions { // Options go here } public void Execute(ActivationContext context, ComponentVars vars) { var options = vars.GetOptions<IMyComponentOptions>(); // Do something with the options } } Any component that utilizes another component should ensure that its own options interface inherits from the options interfaces of its subcomponents. public class MyComponent : IComponent { public interface IMyComponentOptions : SubComponent.ISubComponentOptions { // Options go here // Since this inherits from SubComponent.ISubComponentOptions, it will also contain the options for SubComponent } public void Execute(ActivationContext context, ComponentVars vars) { var options = vars.GetOptions<IMyComponentOptions>(); // Do something with the options new ComponentExecutor(context, vars) .Execute<SubComponent>(); } } Variable Sharing Components can share variables with each other via the ComponentVars object. Some examples of this are vars.GetPoints() and vars.GetTargets<T>(). These are populated by another component, the GetTargetsComponent. The only caveat is, any components that use variables populated by another component must be executed after that component. public class MyComponent : IComponent { public void Execute(ActivationContext context, ComponentVars vars) { var targets = vars.GetTargets<Creature>(); // Do something with the targets } } If you create a component that needs to share variables with other components, you should create methods inside ComponentVars to store and retrieve that information. Scripting Components are intended to be used in scripts. The first step is to know what components you will be utilizing within your script. For example, if you want to create a script that plays a body animation, gets targets, deals damage to those targets, animates those targets, and plays a sound, it might look something like this. public class DamageScript : ConfigurableSpellScriptBase, BodyAnimationComponent.IBodyAnimationComponentOptions, GetTargetsComponent<Creature>.IGetTargetsComponentOptions, DamageComponent.IDamageComponentOptions, AnimationComponent.IAnimationComponentOptions, SoundComponent.ISoundComponentOptions { public override OnUse(SpellContext context) => new ComponentExecutor(context) .WithOptions(this) .Execute<BodyAnimationComponent>() .ExecuteAndCheck<GetTargetsComponent<Creature>>() ? .Execute<DamageComponent>() .Execute<AnimationComponent>() .Execute<SoundComponent>(); #region ScriptVars // All of the combined options from the component options interfaces #endregion } In the above example, the script is utilizing 5 components. Each of those components has an options interface that the script inherits from. The script also inherits from the ConfigurableSpellScriptBase. More about this script base can be found in the Scripting article. Another important thing to notice is that the GetTargetsComponent<T> is an IConditionalComponent. These types of components are executed via ExecuteAndCheck<TComponent>(). If the conditional part of the component fails, it causes the execution of ExecuteAndCheck<TComponent>() to return null. By using the Save Navigation Operator (?), we can cause all of the method invocations that come after it to not occur if it returns null.

articles/Dialogs.html.title: Dialogs | Chaos Server Docs
articles/Dialogs.html.keywords: Dialogs Dialogs in Chaos are both templated and scripted objects. When you define a dialog, you are defining a template that will be used to create new instances of that dialog as requested. Each dialog that is displays is a fresh instance of that dialog created from it's template. Dialog Templates A dialog template is used to create new instances of dialogs as required. Any action that brings up a new dialog, whether it be clicking an NPC, or clicking Next or Previous on an existing dialog, the next dialog shown will be a fresh instance of a dialog created from a template. How do I create them? By default, dialog templates should be created in Data\Configuration\Templates\Dialogs. Configuration of how map templates are loaded can be found in appsettings.json at Options:DialogTemplateCacheOptions. Map templates are initially serialized into DialogTemplateSchema before being mapped to a DialogTemplate. The schema object is mapped via the DialogMapperProfile. See DialogTemplateSchema for a list of all configurable properties with descriptions. How do I use them? Dialogs can be created by using the DialogFactory, which is an implementation of [IDialogFactory](<xref:Chaos.Services.Factories.Abstractions.IDialogFactory). Note Each dialog is a fresh instance of a dialog created from a template. Any changes made to the template will apply to all instances of that dialog. private readonly IDialogFactory DialogFactory; public Foo(IDialogFactory dialogFactory) => DialogFactory = dialogFactory; public void Bar() { //create a new dialog with dialogSource as the source of the dialog //dialogSource can be almost any object (items, monsters, merchants, aislings, etc) //extraScriptKeys is optional, and can be used to pass in extra script keys that are not part of the templated dialog var dialog = DialogFactory.Create("myDialogTemplateKey", dialogSource, extraScriptKeys); } State and Context Dialogs can have context passed between them by setting the Contextual property of the dialog template to true. This will cause any dialog leading to a contextual dialog to pass it's state to it. State is stored in 2 places, Dialog.MenuArgs and Dialog.Context. Note You can create chains of dialogs that share context by specifying Contextual on all dialogs in that chain except the first one Caution Do not specify Contextual on a dialog that is the first in a chain of dialogs that share context Dialog.MenuArgs MenuArgs is an ArgumentCollection. This is used when specifying a dialog type of MenuTextEntry, MenuTextEntryWithArgs, and DialogTextEntry. When the user types in input and clicks ok, MenuArgs will be populated with what they typed in. The ArgumentCollection facilitates easier conversion of the input to the desired type. For example, if you want the user to type in a number, ArgumentCollection can perform that conversion for you. Caution A dialog type 'WithArgs' can have only 1 argument passed into it from MenuArgs. If the dialog has a type 'TextEntry', the client can only return a maximum of 2 arguments, one passed in and one user input, with the last one always being the most recent input Dialog.Context Context is just an object, otherwise known as a Tag. This is meant to be used as context for dialog scripts. The type of object stored is up to the script, and any script receiving that context should know what that object is. This should rarely be used, only in situations where there you need more than the 2 arguments MenuArgs can provide. When a dialogs Context property is passed to the next dialog, it is deep cloned just in case there is a need to use that specific snapshot of the context later on.
